metadata.title: Self-Distilled Reasoner: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Large Language Models

anchors.claim: SFT suffers from exposure bias and off-policy mismatch; RLVR suffers from high sampling cost and sparse reward.
anchors.evidence: Introduction and Background discuss limitations of RLVR and SFT.

anchors.claim: GRPO uses group-normalized binary rewards and sequence-level advantage.
anchors.evidence: Eq. 4-5 define GRPO advantage and objective.

anchors.claim: GRPO can stagnate when reward std within a group is zero.
anchors.evidence: Introduction and Figure 3 discussion mention reward diversity collapse.

anchors.claim: OPSD instantiates student and teacher from the same model with different conditioning context.
anchors.evidence: Section 3 and Figure 2 explain p_S(.|x) and p_T(.|x,y*).

anchors.claim: Teacher uses privileged information from the reference solution to supervise the student rollout.
OPSD minimizes per-token divergence between teacher and student along the student rollout.
Teacher rationalization is implicit and does not require explicit generation of a teacher answer.
OPSD can be interpreted as dense-reward policy gradient with token-level reward based on log-prob difference.

anchors.label: Table 1
anchors.claim: OPSD combines on-policy data, dense learning signal, low sampling cost, and no external teacher.

anchors.label: Figure 1
anchors.claim: Overview of OPSD showing shared-model teacher/student with per-token divergence over student rollout.

anchors.label: Figure 2
anchors.claim: Prompt example for student and teacher policies with privileged reference solution.

anchors.label: Figure 3
anchors.claim: OPSD is more token-efficient than GRPO and avoids reward diversity collapse.

anchors.label: Table 2
anchors.claim: OPSD outperforms SFT and matches or exceeds GRPO across Qwen3 model sizes on math benchmarks.

anchors.label: Table 3
anchors.claim: Forward KL outperforms reverse KL and JSD.

anchors.label: Figure 4
anchors.claim: Per-token pointwise KL clipping prevents collapse.

anchors.label: Table 4
anchors.claim: Full-vocabulary logit distillation outperforms sampled-token distillation.

anchors.label: Table 5
anchors.claim: TM-off student with TM-on teacher yields strongest KL on math tokens.

anchors.label: Table 6
anchors.claim: GRPO uses 8 generations and 16k length, while OPSD uses 1 generation and 1024 length under the reported setup.
